_meta.purpose: Curated overlay linking recorded DPWH awardees to official actions and court filings. Primary-source-or-omit: every entry carries a source link. Firms with no confirmed official action carry recorded facts only.
_meta.disclaimer: All data sourced from public records (COA, SEC, DBM, PSA, BSP, SALN disclosures) and named court/agency filings. This tool computes statistical indicators only. Charges are allegations under the presumption of innocence; specific allegations, if any, require independent investigation and corroboration.
_meta.verification_note: Each action below was cross-checked against a primary or primary-citing source (PCAB Board Resolution 075, Ombudsman/Sandiganbayan filings, DPWH Secretary orders, COA fraud audit reports, SEC resolutions). Rappler and Philstar block automated fetching; where they were the only source, the fact was confirmed against a second outlet or Wikipedia's cited references.

pcab075_inq.label: Inquirer: 9 Discaya firms stripped of contractor license (PCAB Board Resolution 075)

pcab075_gma.label: GMA: PCAB revokes licenses of 9 Sarah Discaya-owned firms

sec_gma.label: GMA: SEC revokes corporate registrations of St. Timothy, St. Gerrard

sunwest_gma_file.label: GMA: Sandiganbayan raffles off cases vs Zaldy Co, others over flood control mess

sunwest_gma_charge.label: GMA: Ombudsman charges Zaldy Co over flood project anomalies (Naujan, Oriental Mindoro)

wawao_mt.label: Manila Times: DPWH Sec. Dizon orders perpetual ban of Wawao Builders, SYMS Construction for ghost projects

topnotch_wiki.label: Wikipedia: Flood control projects scandal (COA fraud audit flags Topnotch Barangay Bulihan riverbank project, ~P69M)

topnotch_doj.label: Inquirer: DOJ issues lookout bulletin vs individuals in flood mess

scandal_wiki.label: Wikipedia: Flood control projects scandal in the Philippines

investigation.chair: Ret. SC Assoc. Justice Andres Reyes Jr.
investigation.note: Investigated flagged flood-control projects; referred individuals to the Ombudsman and DOJ. Final report held pending release decision.
Subpoenaed the highest-volume contractors; Sarah Discaya testified Sept 1, 2025.
Filed the first criminal charges before the Sandiganbayan (Nov 2025).
Issued fraud audit reports on Bulacan flood-control projects.

context.top15_note: News reporting and the Marcos administration's review found that about 15 contractors received roughly PHP 100 billion, or around 20% of flood-control program spending. Being a high-volume awardee is a descriptive fact from the records, not an allegation.

persons.name: Cezarah "Sarah" Discaya
persons.role: Admitted owner/officer of nine construction firms (Senate testimony, Sept 1, 2025)
persons.status: Firms' PCAB contractor licenses revoked (Board Resolution 075, Sept 2025)

persons.name: Curlee Discaya
persons.status: Firms' PCAB contractor licenses revoked (Board Resolution 075, Sept 2025)

persons.name: Elizaldy "Zaldy" Co
persons.role: Resigned representative; linked to Sunwest, Inc.
persons.status: Charged by the Ombudsman before the Sandiganbayan (graft, malversation) over the Naujan flood-control project, Nov 2025. Charges are allegations; case pending.

persons.name: Eumir and Pamela Villanueva
persons.role: Owners of Topnotch Catalyst Builders Inc.
persons.status: Named on the DOJ immigration lookout bulletin order (Sept 2025, news-reported). A lookout order is investigative, not a charge.

The Ombudsman charged company directors and Zaldy Co before the Sandiganbayan (graft under RA 3019 sec. 3.e and 3.h, and malversation) over the PHP 289.5-million Naujan, Oriental Mindoro flood-control project. Cases were distributed to the 5th, 6th and 7th Divisions (Nov 2025). Charges are allegations; the case is pending.
DPWH ordered perpetual disqualification for ghost flood-control projects in Bulacan (DPWH Secretary order)
DPWH ordered perpetual disqualification for ghost flood-control projects in Bulacan (DPWH Secretary order)
Flagged in a COA fraud audit over a riverbank protection project at Barangay Bulihan, Bulacan (about PHP 69 million). Flagged for review; not a finding of guilt.
Owners named on the DOJ immigration lookout bulletin order (investigative, not a charge)
